step_by_step_thinking:
The consensus among agents has been consistent in identifying that while decreased FEV1, MEFR, and increased residual volume (RV) are all hallmarks of COPD, decreased diffusion capacity reflects a different pathophysiological condition typically seen in conditions affecting the alveolar-capillary barrier such as interstitial lung disease. Therefore, I concur with the group assessment that option D correctly identifies something outside the scope of COPD-specific presentations. The unique nature of decreased diffusion capacity makes it stand out as non-characteristic of COPD.

Final
raw_response:
The consensus among agents has been consistent in identifying that while decreased FEV1, MEFR, and increased residual volume (RV) are all hallmarks of COPD, decreased diffusion capacity reflects a different pathophysiological condition typically seen in conditions affecting the alveolar-capillary barrier such as interstitial lung disease. Therefore, I concur with the group assessment that option D correctly identifies something outside the scope of COPD-specific presentations. The unique nature of decreased diffusion capacity makes it stand out as non-characteristic of COPD.

Final Answer: D
